name: Frozen Pumpkin Mousse
recipeIngredient: 1 cup WHIPPING CREAM, beaten until stiff peaks form
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan, bring water and sugar to a boil. Continue boiling until the syrup reaches a soft ball stage (238°F) on a candy thermometer.
2. While the syrup is boiling, use an electric mixer to beat egg whites with a pinch of cream of tartar until stiff peaks form.
3. With the mixer running, slowly pour the hot sugar syrup into the egg whites in a steady stream. Continue beating until the mixture is completely cool (this may take more than 10 minutes).
4. Gently fold in the pumpkin puree and pie spice.
5. Beat the rum into the whipped cream and gently fold it into the pumpkin mixture.
6. Transfer the mousse to a souffle dish that has been fitted with a paper collar. Freeze for at least 4 hours.
7. Before serving, remove the mousse from the freezer and place it in the refrigerator for about 30 minutes. Spoon into dessert dishes and serve with gingersnap cookies.
